title: 2013 forty billion dollar share repurchase authorization
decisionQuestion: Should the company proceed with the approved $40 billion share repurchase program?
situation: The board of directors has approved a new share repurchase program.
conditions: The board has formally approved the program.
The authorization limit is set at $40 billion.
constraints: The repurchase must adhere to the $40 billion limit.
The decision must align with shareholder return goals.

options.description: Execute the full $40 billion share repurchase authorization.
options.expectedBenefit: Maximize shareholder return and signal strong confidence.
options.principalRisk: Potential overpayment or market timing risk.

options.description: Execute a partial repurchase, utilizing less than $40 billion.
options.expectedBenefit: Maintain flexibility for future strategic investments.
options.principalRisk: Signal weaker confidence or missed opportunity.

chosenAction: Execute the full $40 billion share repurchase authorization.
rejectedBenefit: Maintaining flexibility for future strategic investments.
observations: The board approved a new share repurchase program.
The authorization limit is up to $40 billion.
inferences: Executing the full program maximizes the immediate return signal to shareholders.
A partial repurchase suggests the company may be reserving capital for other uses.

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: The board of directors also approved a new share repurchase program authorizing up to $40 billion in share repurchases.
